config.title: SQL Snippet Reference
config.subtitle: A searchable collection of essential SQL patterns

snippets.title: SELECT all
snippets.description: Retrieves all columns and rows from a table.

snippets.label: Standard
snippets.code: SELECT * FROM table_name;

snippets.title: SELECT specific columns
snippets.description: Retrieves only the specified columns from a table.

snippets.label: Standard
snippets.code:
SELECT col1, col2, col3
FROM table_name;

snippets.title: SELECT distinct
snippets.description: Retrieves only unique values from a column.

snippets.label: Single column
snippets.code:
SELECT DISTINCT column_name
FROM table_name;

snippets.label: Multiple columns
snippets.code:
SELECT DISTINCT col1, col2
FROM table_name;

snippets.title: WHERE clause
snippets.description: Filters rows based on a condition.

snippets.label: Equals
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE column_name = 'value';

snippets.label: Multiple conditions
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE col1 = 'value' AND col2 > 10;

snippets.title: ORDER BY
snippets.description: Sorts the result set in ascending or descending order.

snippets.label: Ascending
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
ORDER BY column_name ASC;

snippets.label: Descending
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
ORDER BY column_name DESC;

snippets.label: Multiple columns
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
ORDER BY col1 ASC, col2 DESC;

snippets.title: LIMIT / TOP
snippets.description: Restricts the number of rows returned.

snippets.label: MySQL / PostgreSQL
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
LIMIT 10;

snippets.label: SQL Server
snippets.code:
SELECT TOP 10 *
FROM table_name;

snippets.title: LIKE (pattern match)
snippets.description: Filters rows where a column matches a pattern.

snippets.label: Starts with
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE name LIKE 'A%';

snippets.label: Contains
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE name LIKE '%word%';

snippets.label: Ends with
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE name LIKE '%Z';

snippets.title: IN clause
snippets.description: Filters rows matching any value in a list.

snippets.label: Value list
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE col IN ('a', 'b', 'c');

snippets.label: Subquery
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE col IN (SELECT id FROM other_table);

snippets.title: BETWEEN
snippets.description: Filters rows within a range of values.

snippets.label: Numeric range
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE age BETWEEN 18 AND 65;

snippets.label: Date range
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE created_at BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-12-31';

snippets.title: IS NULL / IS NOT NULL
snippets.description: Checks whether a column value is null.

snippets.label: Is null
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE column_name IS NULL;

snippets.label: Is not null
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM table_name
WHERE column_name IS NOT NULL;

snippets.title: INNER JOIN
snippets.description: Returns rows with matching values in both tables.

snippets.label: Basic

snippets.title: LEFT JOIN
snippets.description: Returns all rows from the left table, matched rows from right.

snippets.label: Basic
Unmatched rows only
snippets.code:
SELECT A.*
FROM TableA A
LEFT JOIN TableB B ON A.id = B.a_id
WHERE B.a_id IS NULL;

snippets.title: RIGHT JOIN
snippets.description: Returns all rows from the right table, matched rows from left.

snippets.label: Basic

snippets.title: FULL OUTER JOIN
snippets.description: Returns all rows when there is a match in either table.

snippets.label: Basic

snippets.title: SELF JOIN
snippets.description: Joins a table to itself, useful for hierarchical data.

snippets.label: Employee manager

snippets.title: CROSS JOIN
snippets.description: Returns the Cartesian product of two tables.

snippets.label: Basic

snippets.title: GROUP BY
snippets.description: Groups rows sharing a property to apply aggregate functions.

snippets.label: Basic
With HAVING
snippets.code:
SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM employees
GROUP BY department
HAVING cnt > 5;

snippets.title: Aggregate functions
snippets.description: Compute summary values across rows.

snippets.label: Common aggregates
snippets.code:
SELECT
  COUNT(*)    AS total_rows,
  SUM(salary) AS total_salary,
  AVG(salary) AS avg_salary,
  MIN(salary) AS min_salary,
  MAX(salary) AS max_salary
FROM employees;

snippets.title: Window — RANK / ROW_NUMBER
snippets.description: Assigns ranks or sequential numbers to rows within a partition.

snippets.label: ROW_NUMBER
RANK & DENSE_RANK
snippets.code:
SELECT name, salary,
  RANK()       OVER (ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rnk,
  DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY salary DESC) AS dense_rnk
FROM employees;

snippets.title: Window — LAG / LEAD
snippets.description: Accesses values from previous or next rows in the result set.

snippets.label: LAG
LEAD

snippets.title: Window — running total
snippets.description: Computes a cumulative sum over an ordered partition.

snippets.label: Running total

snippets.title: CTE (Common Table Expression)
snippets.description: Defines a named temporary result set for use within a query.

snippets.label: Basic CTE
Multiple CTEs

snippets.title: Recursive CTE
snippets.description: Traverses hierarchical or graph-like data recursively.

snippets.label: Org hierarchy
snippets.code:
WITH RECURSIVE org AS (
  SELECT id, name, manager_id, 1 AS lvl
  FROM employees WHERE manager_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT e.id, e.name, e.manager_id, o.lvl + 1
  FROM employees e
  JOIN org o ON e.manager_id = o.id
)
SELECT * FROM org ORDER BY lvl;

snippets.title: Subquery
snippets.description: Embeds one query inside another.

snippets.label: In WHERE
snippets.code:
SELECT * FROM employees
WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees);

snippets.label: Correlated

snippets.title: CASE expression
snippets.description: Adds conditional logic inside a query.

snippets.label: Simple CASE
snippets.code:
SELECT name,
  CASE grade
    WHEN 'A' THEN 'Excellent'
    WHEN 'B' THEN 'Good'
    ELSE 'Needs improvement'
  END AS performance
FROM students;

snippets.label: Searched CASE
snippets.code:
SELECT name, salary,
  CASE
    WHEN salary >= 100000 THEN 'High'
    WHEN salary >= 50000  THEN 'Mid'
    ELSE 'Low'
  END AS band
FROM employees;

snippets.title: UNION / UNION ALL
snippets.description: Combines result sets from two or more SELECT statements.

snippets.label: UNION (distinct)
UNION ALL

snippets.title: INSERT
snippets.description: Adds new rows to a table.

snippets.label: Single row
snippets.code:
INSERT INTO table_name (col1, col2)
VALUES ('value1', 42);

snippets.label: Multiple rows
snippets.code:
INSERT INTO table_name (col1, col2)
VALUES
  ('a', 1),
  ('b', 2),
  ('c', 3);

snippets.label: Insert from SELECT

snippets.title: UPDATE
snippets.description: Modifies existing rows in a table.

snippets.label: Basic
Update with JOIN
snippets.code:
UPDATE employees e
JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
SET e.salary = e.salary * 1.1
WHERE d.name = 'Engineering';

snippets.title: DELETE
snippets.description: Removes rows from a table.

snippets.label: With condition
snippets.code:
DELETE FROM table_name
WHERE column_name = 'value';

snippets.label: Delete with subquery

snippets.title: CREATE TABLE
snippets.description: Creates a new table in the database.

snippets.label: Basic
With foreign key

snippets.title: ALTER TABLE
snippets.description: Modifies an existing table structure.

snippets.label: Add column
snippets.code:
ALTER TABLE table_name
ADD COLUMN new_col VARCHAR(100);

snippets.label: Drop column
snippets.code:
ALTER TABLE table_name
DROP COLUMN col_name;

snippets.label: Rename column
snippets.code:
ALTER TABLE table_name
RENAME COLUMN old_name TO new_name;

snippets.title: CREATE INDEX
snippets.description: Creates an index to speed up queries on a column.

snippets.label: Basic index
snippets.code: CREATE INDEX idx_col ON table_name (column_name);

snippets.label: Unique index
snippets.code: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_email ON users (email);

snippets.label: Composite index
snippets.code: CREATE INDEX idx_name_dept ON employees (last_name, dept_id);

snippets.title: CREATE VIEW
snippets.description: Creates a virtual table based on a SELECT query.

snippets.label: Basic view
snippets.code:
CREATE VIEW active_users AS
SELECT id, name, email
FROM users
WHERE active = 1;

snippets.title: Transactions
snippets.description: Groups statements into an atomic unit of work.

snippets.label: Basic transaction
With rollback

snippets.title: EXISTS / NOT EXISTS
snippets.description: Tests whether a subquery returns any rows.

snippets.label: EXISTS
NOT EXISTS

snippets.title: PIVOT / conditional aggregation
snippets.description: Transforms row values into columns.

snippets.label: Conditional aggregation
snippets.code:
SELECT
  month,
  SUM(CASE WHEN product = 'A' THEN sales ELSE 0 END) AS product_a,
  SUM(CASE WHEN product = 'B' THEN sales ELSE 0 END) AS product_b
FROM sales
GROUP BY month;

snippets.title: Top N per group
snippets.description: Retrieves the top N rows for each group.

snippets.label: Using ROW_NUMBER

snippets.title: String functions
snippets.description: Commonly used functions for text manipulation.

snippets.label: MySQL / PostgreSQL
snippets.code:
SELECT
  UPPER(name)              AS upper_name,
  LOWER(name)              AS lower_name,
  LENGTH(name)             AS name_len,
  SUBSTRING(name, 1, 3)    AS first_3,
  CONCAT(first, ' ', last) AS full_name,
  TRIM('  hello  ')        AS trimmed
FROM users;

snippets.title: Date functions
snippets.description: Commonly used functions for working with dates.

snippets.label: MySQL
snippets.code:
SELECT
  NOW()                       AS current_datetime,
  CURDATE()                   AS today,
  YEAR(created_at)            AS yr,
  MONTH(created_at)           AS mo,
  DATEDIFF(NOW(), created_at) AS days_old
FROM users;

snippets.label: PostgreSQL
snippets.code:
SELECT
  NOW(),
  CURRENT_DATE,
  EXTRACT(YEAR FROM created_at)   AS yr,
  AGE(NOW(), created_at)          AS age,
  DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) AS month_start
FROM users;

snippets.title: COALESCE / NULLIF
snippets.description: Handles NULL values in expressions.

snippets.label: COALESCE
snippets.code:
SELECT name, COALESCE(phone, 'N/A') AS phone
FROM contacts;

snippets.label: NULLIF
snippets.code: SELECT NULLIF(col, 0) AS safe_col FROM table_name;
